programme.desc: Matt, Lou, and Lyndah try to repo a car from a man who is not afraid to throw some punches. Then, Matt and Lou face a dilemma when they realize their target vehicle is filled with angry bees. And, Sonia and Froy hit the airwaves to reclaim a truck that's being used in a radio broadcast stunt.

programme.title: Burning Up
programme.desc: Lou's new business relationship with Matt starts to unravel when a botched job puts a serious strain on the company.

programme.title: Heart Attack Panic
programme.desc: Lou and Sonia are caught in a desperate situation when the owner of their target car is struck by a heart attack. Plus, a repo becomes violent under the watchful eye of the finance company. FSC_OperationRepo_S9E11_HD

programme.title: Crunch Time
programme.desc: Sonia and Froy interrupt a debtor and two maids who are getting hands-on with their job. A confrontation with construction workers goes bad, and somebody's vehicle will get destroyed.

programme.title: The Big Letdown
programme.desc: Froy meets with a lawyer, but his legal troubles are worse than expected. Sonia and Froy confront a debtor who uses a homeless man in his ploy to keep his car. Then, Sonia and Froy discover one debtor's alcoholism is only a farce to cover a bigger secret. And finally, the team's attempt to repo fifteen bikes from a gym is not the morale booster Lou had hoped for.

programme.title: The Waiting Game
programme.desc: While Froy waits on news from his lawyer about his warrant in Texas, he considers taking matters into his own hands. Lou and Froy bust some slick moves in an attempt to wrangle a car from a debtor who is prepared to battle. Then, when one woman behaves like a child, Matt puts her in timeout, but worries he may have gone too far. And finally, Lou, Matt, and Froy take on an jealous husband with deadly aim.

programme.title: This Is No Fantasy
programme.desc: First, Froy and Lyndah get a tip from a spotter that leads them to a late model sedan with cans tied to the bumper and an angry bride in the passenger side. Next, Matt has to step in when Froy starts to lose it with a group of fantasy football players. And finally, Sonia, Lyndah, Froy, and Lou are astounded when a loopy debtor douses his own car in gasoline and sends it up in flames.

programme.title: Repo, the Final Frontier
programme.desc: First, Froy and Sonia trace an out-of-state skip account to a seedy motel, where they come face-to-face with a hopping-mad cowboy and his female companion. Then, Matt and Sonia go where no repo man has gone before to repossess a Ford from a delusional nerd who may as well actually be from space. And finally, Lou and Lyndah have trouble getting to their car because there's a chainsaw-wielding gardener in the way.

programme.title: Missed Dates
programme.desc: First, Lyndah is put in a tough situation - she has to control a sobbing, cop-calling debtor and turn away Frankie's unwanted and inappropriate advances - all at the same time. Then, Matt and Froy have two problems on their hands, when they decide to haul away both a stripped-down husk of a car and the phone number of its owner's coquettish and bathrobe-wearing ex-girlfriend. Finally, Lou, Froy, and Lyndah head way out into the country to wrangle with a case of mistaken identity and a trio of shotgun-wielding cultists.

programme.title: Pyramid Scams and Princesses
programme.desc: First, Lou, Sonia, and Matt wrangle with two belligerent truckers who will stop at nothing to protect their massive tractor-trailer. Next, a young girl learns that she's been a victim of a pyramid scam when Lyndah and Lou come for her car. Finally, Matt, Sonia, and Lyndah enjoy a little nightlife when they hit the club to take down an entitled princess who's high on charm, but late on her payments. But the debtor's not the only one who fights to get the car.

programme.title: Call for Help
programme.desc: With the pressure of Froy's legal problems mounting, the team does everything they can to convince him to hire a lawyer. Froy and Matt confront a misogynist frat boy with a dark secret, Sonia and Froy wrangle with a squadron of costumed rally racers, and Lou, Matt, and Froy get the surprise of a lifetime when they're faced with a truck-full of vicious gangsters.

programme.title: Hairy Situations
programme.desc: First, things get hairy when Sonia and Lyndah repo from outside a wig shop. Next, everybody's crying when Lou and Froy's repossession from a young couple ends with a pepper spray to everyone's face. And finally, Froy, Sonia, and Lou repo the targeted car while Matt stands his ground with a family of thugs.

programme.title: Look What You Did!
programme.desc: First, Sonia and Froy get locked in the battle of a lifetime with a pair of young debtors who are understandably upset that their late model Tahoe has found its way to the bottom of a lake. Is it Froy's fault, as they claim, or are there other forces at work here? Then, Lou and Matt get down and dirty with a taser-packing P.I. who isn't ready to say goodbye to his ride. Finally, when Frankie puts the go-jacks on the wrong car, it plucks Matt's last nerve and places him at risk for the beat-down of the century.

programme.title: Smoke, Lead, and Alcohol
programme.desc: First, Matt and Froy venture into one of the city's most dangerous neighborhoods, only to find a debtor ready to defend his ride by pumping it full of lead, with no concern for who gets caught in the crossfire.

programme.title: How Do They Do It

programme.title: Engineering the Future
programme.desc: A new era of space, where small satellites no bigger than a showbox will be deployed not in one or twos, but in their thousands, referred to as satellite swarms.

programme.title: Engineering the Future
programme.desc: Virgin Hyperloop is a train that runs inside a steel tunnel. This revolutionary new vehicle floats along the track using magnetic levitation and glides at airline speeds.

programme.title: The Demolition Man

programme.title: The Demolition Man

programme.title: The Demolition Man

programme.title: Oddly Satisfying Science
programme.desc: This week's experiments hit high notes as we discover the physics of theremins, investigate how the outer ear impacts human hearing, and put on a laser show with cymatics.

programme.title: Speed
programme.desc: Throughout history, human society has run on trade. The innovations that allowed faster and more accurate oceanic travel revolutionized the world. New inventions now help people and goods move on the water faster for recreation and commerce.

programme.title: Quick Connections with Alie Ward

programme.title: Speed

programme.title: Wildest Places: Pacific

Get comfortable, because you're about to experience Extreme Weather Countdown.

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown | Let It Snow, Let It Snow, Let It Snow

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Height of Ruin
programme.desc: Adventurer George Kourounis joins Mark and Jaclyn on a journey to Nepal. They witness the devastation caused by the deadly 2015 earthquake, see first-hand the reconstruction efforts, and are awed by the warmth and hospitality of the Nepalese people.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Height of Ruin

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Ferocity in Florida

programme.title: Legends of Design
programme.desc: Some of history's greatest designers share intriguing perspectives and vignettes from their prolific journeys. 1) The joy of shopping -- and how to recapture it online | Nimisha Jain 2) Small rockets are the next space revolution | Peter Beck 3) How does your smartphone know your location? - Wilton L. Virgo 4) A futuristic vision for Latin America, rooted in ancient design | Catalina Lotero 5) Origami robots that reshape and transform themselves | Jamie Paik

programme.title: Riddle Us This
programme.desc: Follow along and solve fun, fascinating puzzles that'll challenge your mind and expand your thinking. 1) How the Königsberg bridge problem changed mathematics - Dan Van der Vieren 2) Sherlock Holmes and the case of the Red-Headed League | Alex Rosenthal 3) Can you outsmart the fallacy that fooled a generation of doctors? | Elizabeth Cox 4) Euclid's puzzling parallel postulate - Jeff Dekofsky 5) Can you solve the wizard tournament riddle? | Alex Rosenthal 6) Can you solve the basketball robot riddle? | Dan Katz 7) Can you outsmart the college admissions fallacy? | Elizabeth Cox 8) Can you solve the time traveling car riddle? | Daniel Finkel 9) Can you solve the risky disk riddle? | James Tanton 10) How to defeat a dragon with math - Garth Sundem 11) Can you solve the secret assassin society riddle? | Alex Rosenthal

programme.title: Say What?
programme.desc: Explore the curious world of language and how it shapes the way we think. (1) The benefits of a bilingual brain - Mia Nacamulli (2) 4 reasons to learn a new language | John McWhorter (3) How language shapes the way we think | Lera Boroditsky (4) What is dyslexia? - Kelli Sandman-Hurley (5) The secrets of learning a new language | Lýdia Machová (6) Where did English come from? - Claire Bowern (7) 3 ways to speak English | Jamila Lyiscott

programme.title: Revolutionary Women!
programme.desc: What happens when purpose and passion overshadow fear? Follow in the steps of these outstanding women who let their ferocity, fortitude and wisdom guide them in living -- and speaking -- their truth. 1) On tennis, love and motherhood | Serena Williams and Gayle King 2) The woman who stared at the sun | Alex Gendler 3) The trauma of systematic racism is killing Black women. A first step toward change... | T. Morgan Dixon and Vanessa Garrison 4) How women will lead us to freedom, justice and peace | H.E. Ellen Johnson Sirleaf

programme.title: Climate Action, Now!
programme.desc: Get inspired to save the world with these dedicated minds and leaders tackling one of the biggest threats against modern civilization: climate change. 1) The secret perks of driving electric | Cynthia Williams 2) What is the tragedy of the commons? - Nicholas Amendolare 3) An urgent call to protect the world's "Third Pole" | Tshering Tobgay 4) Climate action's hidden opportunities for women | Zineb Sqalli 5) The satellite helping slow climate change ‚Äî right now | Millie Chu Baird

programme.title: Art of the Future
programme.desc: What does it look like inside the mind of a machine? See what creativity lies and thrives within, and meet those looking to unleash it. 1) What's an algorithm? - David J. Malan 2) How bad data keeps us from good AI | Mainak Mazumdar 3) Design at the intersection of technology and biology | Neri Oxman 4) Why I draw with robots | Sougwen Chung 5) This app makes it fun to pick up litter | Jeff Kirschner 6) Is there a limit to technological progress? - Clément Vidal

programme.title: The Future of Cities
programme.desc: Discover what's potentially in store for the world's urban hotspots. 1) Lessons from people already adapting to the climate crisis | Dorcas Naishorua 2) How buildings can improve life ‚Äî inside and out | Doris Sung 3) How pigeons took over the world | Elizabeth Carlen and Joanna Moles 4) Meet mini-grids — the clean energy solution bringing power to millions | Tombo Banda 5) We need to track the world's water like we track the weather | Sonaar Luthra 6) How do tornadoes form? - James Spann
